concept: Love / Feel Deep Affection / Care Profoundly
description: To feel and express deep affection, care, and commitment toward another being. 'Lothea' encompasses the full spectrum of love—from tender caring to profound devotion—while emphasizing love as active practice rather than mere feeling: to love is to act with love.
sound_symbolism: The word flows with warmth and openness. 'Lo' reaches with liquid extension, heart extending toward another. Then 'the' /θe/ breathes with gentle intimacy, the sacred softness of connection. Finally 'a' opens fully, love given without reservation.
grammatical_notes: As verb: 'mi ru lothea' (I love you). As noun: 'lo lothea' (the love). Can specify type: 'lo nesi lothea' (familial love), 'lo seri lothea' (peaceful/compassionate love). Related to meropi (cherish) but broader—lothea is the foundation, meropi the treasuring. Used with reciprocal: 'sio wiso lothea' (we love each other).

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Metta (loving-kindness) is love without condition or possession—wishing well for all beings, starting with those close to us.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Love expressed through language creates connection—naming affection strengthens bonds and affirms relationship.
